strengths.title: Category Creation
strengths.description: Defines 'Collaborative Adaptive Nutrition' — a category that doesn't exist yet. First-mover advantage.

strengths.title: Adaptive Shared Cooking Moat
strengths.description: One-prep-two-plates workflow is genuinely difficult to replicate. Addresses a real pain point no one else solves.

strengths.title: Cloudflare-Native Architecture
strengths.description: Zero infrastructure cost at launch. Sub-100ms real-time sync. PWA distribution. Global edge deployment.

strengths.title: Relationship-First UX Philosophy
strengths.description: 'We' framing, shared kitchen metaphor, emotional design — fundamentally different from clinical nutrition apps.

strengths.title: Pantry-First AI
strengths.description: Inverts the traditional recipe → grocery list model. Reduces waste and decisions simultaneously.

strengths.title: Fast Iteration
strengths.description: PWA + single Cloudflare deployment = ship daily. No App Store review process.

weaknesses.title: No Existing User Base
weaknesses.description: Starting from zero. No recipe database, no community, no brand recognition.

weaknesses.title: Complexity Risk
weaknesses.description: The product is ambitious. Balancing power with simplicity is the hardest design challenge.

weaknesses.title: Single-Platform Dependency
weaknesses.description: Everything runs on Cloudflare. If Cloudflare has issues, the product has issues.

weaknesses.title: No Native App Presence
weaknesses.description: PWA is technically capable but some users expect App Store presence for trust.

weaknesses.title: AI Dependency
weaknesses.description: Core value prop requires AI. If Anthropic pricing changes, unit economics shift.

opportunities.title: Underserved Market
opportunities.description: No one owns the center of the Venn diagram. The category is fragmented and converging.

opportunities.title: Growing AI Capabilities
opportunities.description: AI meal generation quality is improving rapidly. The product gets better as models improve.

opportunities.title: Mental Load Conversation
opportunities.description: Cultural awareness of 'mental load' in relationships is growing. Market timing aligns.

opportunities.title: Health-Conscious Couples Trend
opportunities.description: More couples are cooking at home, tracking nutrition, and pursuing fitness goals together.

opportunities.title: PWA Adoption
opportunities.description: iOS 16.4+ added full PWA support including push notifications. Technical barriers disappearing.

opportunities.title: Freemium Model
opportunities.description: Free tier drives adoption; premium tier monetizes the AI layer. Low-risk for users, scalable revenue.

threats.title: Yummo — Direct Moat Overlap
threats.description: Nearly identical positioning: 'One cooking session — two plates.' Lacks real-time collaboration and relationship UX.

threats.title: Fitia Convergence
threats.description: Actively adding shared features and could pivot to relationship-first UX. 10M+ existing users.

threats.title: PlateMates — Concept Similarity
threats.description: 'One Meal, Two Ways' split-plate concept for dietary differences. 200+ recipes.

threats.title: Large Players Entering
threats.description: Samsung Food, MyFitnessPal, or similar could add collaborative features. They have resources and distribution.

risks.mitigation: Own relationship UX + pantry-first first; build community
Speed to market; emotional positioning first
Strict 3-phase rollout; Phase 1 is just collaboration
Invite code is frictionless; push notifications pull them in
AI Gateway caching; $10/mo covers costs with margin
Social media-driven acquisition; PWA install prompt
Complex workflows are hard to copy; UX philosophy is embedded
Focus on couples is a moat; big companies move slowly
